hero.description: Build tickers, lower thirds, score widgets, bugs, and stingers — bind live data, animate on a visual timeline, and export as HTML overlays ready for air.

items: Your designer finished the graphics. Your operator needs them on air in 20 minutes. Tweenly handles the middle part.
No plugins were harmed in the making of this broadcast.
Fun fact: the lower third you're looking at right now was built in Tweenly.
One more "can you just make the logo bigger" away from switching to Tweenly.
Tweenly + Stream Circle — from playlist to pixel, no exports in between.
HTML overlays that work offline. Yes, really. ZIP it and forget the WiFi.
Your scoreboard data changes. Your graphic updates. Nobody had to alt-tab.
30 years in broadcast taught us one thing — operators don't have time for workarounds.

brand.intro: Design a complete on-air look — every lower third, ticker, score widget, and bug coordinated as one brand system. Build a new channel from scratch, or extend your existing graphics package without breaking visual consistency.

kits.name: KICK
kits.tagline: Live sports. Real-time data. High energy.

newChannels.label: For new channels
newChannels.heading: Launch a brand-new channel
newChannels.description: Start from a blank canvas. Define your colors, type, and motion language — then design a complete graphics package inside one workspace.

existingBrands.label: For existing brands
existingBrands.heading: Extend your current branding
existingBrands.description: Already have a brand book? Send us your existing design files and we'll rebuild them in Tweenly — pixel-perfect, animatable, and production-ready.

solution.description: The visual design tool — a keyframe canvas where every element maps to an HTML overlay layer. Build at your real output resolution, animate on a timeline, bind live data, and ship as HTML or a self-contained package.

onair.leadStrong: Adjust your broadcast while on air.
onair.leadRest: On Air is a separate Tweenly app — the operator's view. Prep graphics in Standby, send them live with one command, edit text right up to the cut. All in the browser, no install.

caps.title: Standby → Live
caps.desc: A two-column control room. Drop a graphic into Standby, hit IN to send it live, OUT to pull it back.

caps.title: Edit fields in Standby
caps.desc: Update names, scores, headlines — directly in Standby, with live preview. Once it’s on air, it’s locked. No accidents.

caps.title: Full playback control
caps.desc: Play, Pause, Replay, jump to markers, trigger Outro — full transport control for every animated graphic.

caps.title: Run as many sessions as you have events
caps.desc: Switch between concurrent sessions in a single browser tab. Each session connects to its own data sources — scoreboards, rundown systems, or manual operator input.

onair.devTitle: JavaScript API for full programmatic control
onair.devDesc: Drive On Air from your automation or scoreboard system. Set text, swap images, trigger play / next / outro from code.

streamcircle.intro: When graphics and playout share the same runtime, live production gets faster and easier to manage. Tweenly integrates directly with Stream Circle — no file exports or middleware. Graphics attach to playlists, controlled from the same interface your operators already use.
streamcircle.tweenlyDesc: HTML5 broadcast graphics with live data connectivity
streamcircle.benefit0Desc: Attach graphics directly to scheduled events and running playlists.
streamcircle.benefit1Desc: Update graphics dynamically from live data sources and automation triggers.
streamcircle.benefit2Desc: From design to on air, no exports between systems.
streamcircle.benefit3Desc: Native integration — Tweenly is a first-class Stream Circle module.
streamcircle.module0Desc: Organize files with metadata and queries.
streamcircle.module1Desc: Plan templates, dynamic blocks, file checking.
streamcircle.module2Desc: Multichannel server with live playlist control.
streamcircle.ctaHeading: Run your channel with Stream Circle.
streamcircle.ctaBody: TV channel automation and playout — scheduling, content management, and live graphics in one system. Built by the same team behind Tweenly.

pricing.heading: Free to Start.
pricing.subheading: Upgrade when you need API control and team collaboration. Per user, per month. No credit card required.

services.paragraph: Already have a finished design? We turn your source files into animatable HTML overlays — ready for Stream Circle playout or any system that supports HTML graphics.

steps.desc: Send us your source files — PSD, AI, video compositing projects, or any standard design format.

steps.title: We rebuild it in HTML
steps.desc: Our team recreates your graphic as a pixel-perfect, animatable HTML overlay.

steps.title: Deploy to playout

faq.label: FAQ
faq.heading: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.intro: Everything you need to know before getting started.

items.q: What is the difference between Editor and On Air?
items.a: Tweenly is a platform with two products. Editor is the design tool — create and animate broadcast graphics visually, no code required. On Air is the live control app — operators load finished graphics, edit fields in Standby, and send them to air. On Air requires graphics built in Editor, but Editor works independently on its own.

items.q: What playout systems does Tweenly work with?
items.a: Tweenly integrates natively with Stream Circle for scheduling and live playout. Exported HTML graphics can also be loaded into any system that supports HTML overlays — via iframe or as a standalone browser source — and controlled through the JavaScript API. With the Unlimited plan, you can export self-contained ZIP packages that run offline in any HTML-compatible playout.

items.q: What output resolutions and frame rates are supported?
items.a: You choose the resolution and frame rate when creating a graphic — Full HD (1920×1080), 4K (3840×2160), or any custom size. Frame rate options are 1 fps, 25 fps, and 50 fps. The exported HTML package matches exactly what you set in the editor.
Tweenly Editor and On Air run in any modern Chromium-based browser (Chrome, Edge, Opera). Firefox and Safari are not officially supported for the editor.
Tweenly runs on Amazon Web Services (AWS) infrastructure. Your graphics, assets, and account data are stored securely in the cloud. We take data protection seriously and comply with EU regulations.

items.q: What happens if I downgrade or cancel my plan?
items.a: Your account and all your graphics remain accessible — nothing gets deleted. You simply return to the Free plan with its feature limits (HTML-only export, no collaboration, no API control in exports). Your work is always yours.

items.q: How long does it take to learn Tweenly?
items.a: Most designers are productive in a few hours. The editor is a visual tool — if you've used any design software, you'll feel at home. On Air is even simpler — operators typically need less than an hour to learn the Standby → Live workflow.

Tweenly Editor and On Air include email support. When deployed together with Stream Circle, support follows the Stream Circle SLA with dedicated response times. We're a small team and we respond fast.

about.heading: 30 years of broadcast software.
about.description: Tweenly is built by PROVYS Technologies — a Prague-based company that has been building mission-critical broadcast software since the early nineties. From scheduling to playout, our tools power TV stations across Europe.

contact.heading: We'd like to hear from you
contact.description: Questions about graphics, playout integration, or On Air setup? Get in touch.

footer.tagline: Design broadcast overlays and control them on air. Built by PROVYS Technologies.
